name: Polyaxon
tagline: Track, schedule, and scale deep learning experiments on Kubernetes
seoTitle: Polyaxon: ML Experiment Tracking on Kubernetes
metaDescription: Polyaxon is an open-source platform to track, schedule, and manage large-scale deep learning experiments on Kubernetes, with a CLI, dashboard, and hyperparameter tuning.
overview: Polyaxon is an open-source platform for building, training, and monitoring deep learning applications at scale. It runs on Kubernetes and deploys into any data center, cloud provider, or a hosted managed service, supporting major frameworks such as TensorFlow, MXNet, Caffe, and Torch.

It is aimed at data science and ML teams that need to track experiments, schedule jobs, and share GPU servers as self-service resources. Polyaxon manages workloads through container and node management, so a team can reproduce runs and keep results organized as the number of experiments grows.

As an experiment-tracking and MLOps tool, Polyaxon covers the full loop: a CLI to run and track experiments, a dashboard to browse projects and runs, plus built-in support for distributed jobs, parallel execution, hyperparameter tuning, and DAG-based pipelines.
features: Tracks logs and resource usage for experiments, with reproducible runs across teams
Runs on Kubernetes and deploys to any data center, cloud provider, or Polyaxon's hosted service
Supports major deep learning frameworks including TensorFlow, MXNet, Caffe, and Torch
Distributed training support for TensorFlow, PyTorch, MPI, Horovod, Spark, and Dask
Hyperparameter tuning with grid search, random search, Hyperband, Bayesian optimization, and Hyperopt
DAG flow engine for running container-native ML pipelines with dependencies between operations

gettingStarted.intro: Install the CLI with pip, deploy Polyaxon onto a Kubernetes cluster, then create a project and run your first experiment.

steps.heading: Install the CLI
steps.body: Install the Polyaxon command-line interface with pip.
steps.code:
# Install Polyaxon CLI
pip install -U polyaxon

steps.heading: Deploy to Kubernetes
steps.body: Create a namespace, add the Helm charts repo, deploy Polyaxon from a config file, and forward the API port.
steps.code:
# Create a namespace
kubectl create namespace polyaxon

# Add Polyaxon charts repo
helm repo add polyaxon https://charts.polyaxon.com

# Deploy Polyaxon
polyaxon admin deploy -f config.yaml

# Access API
polyaxon port-forward

steps.heading: Create a project and run an experiment
steps.body: Create a project, then upload your code and start an experiment while tracking logs and resources.
steps.code:
# Create a project
polyaxon project create --name=quick-start --description='Polyaxon quick start.'

# Upload code and start experiments
polyaxon run -f experiment.yaml -u -l

steps.heading: Open the dashboard
steps.body: Launch the Polyaxon dashboard to browse your projects and experiments.

useCases: Teams sharing GPU servers who need to schedule and queue training jobs as self-service resources
Tracking experiment logs, metrics, and resource usage to keep deep learning runs reproducible
Running hyperparameter searches such as grid, random, Hyperband, or Bayesian optimization at scale
Orchestrating multi-step ML pipelines and distributed training jobs on Kubernetes via DAGs
